steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice 'most effectively uses information from the given sentences to specify the length of the Foucault pendulum's cable.' The correct answer must explicitly state a measurement for the cable length. The relevant note is: 'The pendulum consists of a weighted ball that swings at the end of a roughly 14-meter-long cable.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — With a swing path that appears to change over time, a Foucault pendulum provides evidence of Earth's rotation.: This describes the pendulum's function and apparent behavior but says nothing about the cable length. ✗
  • Choice B — The Foucault pendulum at the University of Oslo consists of a weighted ball and a cable.: This mentions the cable but gives no length measurement, so it does not specify the cable length. ✗
  • Choice C — Although the swing path of a Foucault pendulum doesn't actually change, it appears to change due to Earth rotating beneath the pendulum.: This explains the optical illusion of the pendulum's motion and omits any mention of the cable length. ✗
  • Choice D — The Foucault pendulum at the University of Oslo in Oslo, Norway, includes a cable that is roughly 14 meters long.: This directly states the cable measurement ('roughly 14 meters long'), fulfilling the task. ✓
That matches Choice D — The Foucault pendulum at the University of Oslo in Oslo, Norway, includes a cable that is roughly 14 meters long..
